text:
{
  "title": "Report Granularity and Clipping",
  "group_name": "report_granularity_clipping_signature",
  "family": "measurement_quality",
  "summary": "Represent whether each student's recorded health measurements look self-reported, sensor-like, or clipped at source limits, treating measurement granularity and conformance as risk-adjacent context rather than preprocessing noise.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Use only raw numeric feature values, with the target ignored. For sleep_duration, heart_rate, bmi, calorie_expenditure, step_count, exercise_duration, and water_intake, create feature-specific heaping flags using abs(x - nearest anchor) <= 1e-6: sleep at whole or half hours; heart_rate at multiples of 5 and 10; bmi at whole, half, and one-decimal values; calorie_expenditure at multiples of 50 and 100; step_count at multiples of 500 and 1000; exercise_duration at multiples of 5, 10, and 30; water_intake at 0.25 and 0.5 liter increments. Add exclusive decimal precision tier flags per numeric field: integer, one decimal, two decimals, or finer, then aggregate row-level fractions for coarse-heaped, integer-like, tenth-like, hundredth-like, and fine-grained reports. Fit per-column min, max, and IQR on the training feature table only; for each numeric field add lower-bound hit, upper-bound hit, outside-train-envelope, and boundary_pressure = clip(1 - distance_to_nearest_training_bound / max(0.02 * training_range, 0.05 * IQR, 1e-6), 0, 1), with outside-envelope values assigned pressure 1, then aggregate boundary hit count, outside-envelope count, max pressure, and mean pressure across observed numeric fields. Missing numeric values are skipped in all aggregates; if every numeric field is missing, set aggregate fractions and pressures to 0 and do not add standalone missingness features. Rationale sources: https://mhealth.jmir.org/2021/12/e31618 and https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-67767-3.",
  "expected_signal": "Health tables that mix survey-style and wearable-style signals can contain class-dependent conformance, plausibility, clipping, and rounding artifacts; making granularity and source-boundary pressure explicit may help separate extreme fit or unhealthy profiles from ordinary at-risk rows even when raw values are already present.",
  "risk": "The signal may be an artifact of this generated dataset rather than a stable health mechanism, can be redundant with raw numeric extremes and response-completeness features, and boundary features are unstable if the training min/max are outliers or if implementation accidentally uses target-stratified bounds."
}
